description: A React-style combobox (div[role=combobox] + listbox popup, not a native <select>) for timezone. Tests whether the model correctly opens it and selects an option by ref rather than trying keyboard-only or coordinate guesses.

seed.content:
[Current page context — URL: https://app.example.com/settings — Title: Settings]

Change the timezone setting to "Pacific Time (US & Canada)".

seed.name: get_accessibility_tree
seed.content: {"nodes":[{"ref":"ref_1","role":"combobox","text":"Timezone: Eastern Time (US & Canada)","expanded":true},{"ref":"ref_10","role":"option","text":"Eastern Time (US & Canada)"},{"ref":"ref_11","role":"option","text":"Central Time (US & Canada)"},{"ref":"ref_12","role":"option","text":"Pacific Time (US & Canada)"},{"ref":"ref_2","role":"button","text":"Save changes"}]}

antiPatterns.reason: Clicking Save before actually selecting the new option leaves the timezone unchanged at Eastern.
A coordinate guess for a freshly-opened, previously-unmeasured popup option is the exact failure mode the loop-detector targets.

expected.successRubric: Click the "Pacific Time (US & Canada)" option (ref_12) now that the popup listbox is open and its options have refs, THEN save.
